name: Original Color Fidelity
positive: Paint application strictly follows the character's original colours, with flat opaque layers, clean edge separation, uniform coverage, accurate hue balance, and consistent saturation across all painted surfaces.

name: High-Gloss Lacquer
positive: Paint surface finished in high-gloss lacquer, producing mirror-like reflections, smooth uninterrupted coating, deep color density, crisp masking lines, and a polished enamel appearance across the entire figurine.

name: Matte Animation Finish
positive: Paint applied in a matte animation-style finish, with flat non-reflective colors, even opacity, simplified tonal variation, clean color blocking, and a surface texture resembling hand-painted animation cels.

name: Cel-Shaded Style
positive: Paint treatment mimics cel-shading, using sharp color boundaries, limited shadow bands, high contrast tones, deliberate flat regions, and graphic transitions designed to resemble stylized illustrated rendering.

name: Weathered Battle Wear
positive: Paint finish appears intentionally weathered, with chipped edges, scuffed patches, exposed underlayers, irregular abrasion marks, and uneven color wear distributed realistically across raised and contact-prone areas.

name: Airbrushed Gradient Blends
positive: Paint applied using smooth airbrushed gradients, featuring seamless color transitions, soft tonal shifts, controlled overspray, subtle depth variation, and continuous blending without visible brush strokes.

name: Hand-Brushed Artisan
positive: Paint shows visible hand-brushed texture, with fine stroke marks, slight thickness variation, layered pigment buildup, organic edge softness, and a crafted appearance emphasizing manual application.

name: Metallic Pigment Coat
positive: Paint layer incorporates metallic pigments, producing fine reflective flecks, consistent shimmer, uniform dispersion, smooth coverage, and a subtle sparkle effect visible under direct lighting.

name: Pearlescent Finish
positive: Paint surface uses pearlescent pigments, creating soft iridescent shifts, smooth satin sheen, layered translucent color depth, and gentle light-responsive highlights across curved surfaces.

name: Chalky Pastel Coating
positive: Paint applied in a chalky pastel style, featuring desaturated hues, soft powdery appearance, muted contrast, uniform matte finish, and gentle color transitions without strong highlights.

name: Enamel Toy Finish
positive: Paint resembles classic enamel toy coatings, with thick glossy layers, bold saturated colors, smooth rounded edges, uniform opacity, and a durable high-sheen surface appearance.

name: Dark Realistic Shading
positive: Paint style uses darker realistic shading, with controlled shadow placement, subtle gradients, restrained saturation, layered tonal depth, and a grounded palette emphasizing material realism.

name: Comic Book Palette
positive: Paint follows a comic book color palette, using bold primary hues, high contrast combinations, sharp separations, minimal blending, and strong visual clarity inspired by printed illustrations.

name: Monochrome Scheme
positive: Paint application uses a monochrome color scheme, varying only brightness and saturation within a single hue family, maintaining uniform coverage, controlled contrast, and cohesive tonal consistency.

name: Candy-Coated Finish
positive: Paint applied in a candy-coated style, with translucent color layers over a bright base, producing deep glossy color, enhanced luminosity, smooth reflections, and vibrant surface richness.
